titleStmt.title: A Transcription of John in Codex Carnotensis
VL33
The New Testament
The Gospel according to John
john
titleStmt.funder: The Arts and Humanities Research Council

sponsor.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing

publisher.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing
University of Birmingham

availability.p: Licensed under a Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International License (CC BY 4.0): available for re-use provided attribution is made to the original creators (the IGNTP and any named participants) and an indication is given of any changes made. See further .

surrogates.bibl: Black and white photographs in ITSEE, supplied by Vetus Latina-Institut.

listBibl.bibl: Missing page supplied as an electronic file by the BNF.

projectDesc.p: This transcription was made by the Verbum Project as part of an online and printed edition of the Old Latin versions of John between 2003 and 2010.

editorialDecl.p: This transcription covers the whole manuscript. It is lacunose in John 6:42-57 and 21:7-24. The bulk of the manuscript appears to be written by the same hand, but folios 1-4 and 63 are by a much less regular hand (more difficult to read and with non-standard orthography), and do not use the full extent of the parchment. Folios 192 and 195 are in a more artistic style, probably by a third hand. It is possible that these pages were written by ecclesiastical dignitaries. Some of the manuscript has been reinked. There are a number of digraphs at the ends of lines for combinations such as 'nt', 'ns', 'us', 'ur' etc.. There are very few abbreviations, all of which are recorded. We believe that this is the first full transcription of this manuscript. The transcription includes foliation, lineation, all digraphs, punctuation and abbreviations. The tight binding of the manuscript means that hanging lines are not always visible. Quire signatures, where visible, have been noted. The initial transcription file was a plain text file for use with COLLATE. Further information about the procedures followed may be found in the electronic edition at
This second edition of the file was converted to XML by Catherine Smith using the project's Python conversion scripts 1.0.
The XML specifications used are version 1.5 of the IGNTP Guidelines available at

revisionDesc.change: Adjustment of transcription to version 1.5 of the XML by Hugh Houghton.
Reconversion of the original plain text file using Python scripts to version 1.4 of the XML by Catherine Smith and manual checking.
Hyphen entity replaced by lb attribute break="no" by Hugh Houghton
Change to verse division of 5:18-19 by Hugh Houghton
Transcription converted to the TEI XML schema and corrected for parsing by Hugh Houghton

ab.note: There is an initial blank page at the head of the volume (not numbered) followed by a later insertion explaining that the codex was found in a reliquary and that the rearrangement of quires for binding may show that some pages are missing.
The text on this first page is largely illegible.
This page has a large chi-rho symbol with alpha and omega.
sic
It is possible that 'credunt' ends in a 'nt' digraph, which has extended over the 'i' before 'in'; cf. 'sunt' in 1:13.
The word 'non' appears to be written under 'uoluntate'; it may be that an extra line has been added interlinearly, or bleed through.
The next two lines are illegible and may have been erased.
This page appears to have been erased and is mostly illegible. It should be observed that when the writing picks up, the hand changes to a much more regular and evenly-spaced script.
A new hand takes over at this point, which is the copyist for the majority of the manuscript.
There is a note in the right margin which has not been transcribed.
There is a note in the left margin which has not been transcribed.

ab.w:
res
There is a note in the left margin which has not been transcribed.ponsum

ab.note: There is a note in the left margin which has not been transcribed.
There is a note in the right margin which has not been transcribed.
There is a note in the right margin which has not been transcribed.
Cross in top margin.
sic
A later hand seems to have written the letter 'b' above the 'p' of 'piscina'.

ab.w:
ui
There is a change of hand: the scribe of the first four pages appears to resume for 64r and 64v.
tae

ab.note: Only the first four lines of fol 64v appear to be written, and the remainder is blank.

ab.w:
ue
The principal hand resumes from the beginning of 65r.
rum

ab.note: There is a gap between the 'q' and 'u' of 'quia' which appears to be due to a flaw in the parchment.
There is a black cross here.
The 'e' of the following 'et' may have been erased in order to read 'descendit'.
See note on previous verse.
The pages containing the rest of John 6:42-57 were missing when the manuscript was paginated.
The word 'eis' may possibly be read after 'dixit'; the images are unclear.
Quire signature xii at bottom of page.
Quire signature xiii at foot of page.
Quire signature xiiii at foot of page.
This page appears to have been re-inked.

ab.w:
men
Quire signature xui at foot of page.
dicus

ab.note: The correction is unclear.

ab.w:
sae
Quire signature at foot of page; probably xuii.
culo

ab.note: Black cross.
Illegible quire signature at foot of page (should be xuiii).
Black cross.
There are ink marks after 'palam', which may be bleed through.
Quire signature xuiiii at foot of page.
'uidet' appears to project into the margin.
The margin after 'ma' appears to be blank; there is no sign of 'gis' for 'magister'.

ab.w:
quo
Very faint quire signature at foot of page, presumably xx

ab.note: The ink marks on the rest of the line are bleedthrough from 'me sequatur' on the recto.
Black cross in gap.
fol 192r and fol 192v have been written by a different hand.
The hand of fol. 192 resumes, beginning the text slightly lower down the page than the principal hand.
The principal hand resumes.
Quire signature at foot of page.
Black cross.
The illegible letters 'i' and 'u' can be read on the opposite, page, where they have been transferred by the pages sticking together (and vice versa).
It is not clear whether or not there is another word at the end of this line.
Quire signature at foot of page.
The 'tem' of 'autem' has been rewritten in larger letters.
'scribsit' has been overwritten in larger letters.
Quire signature at foot of page.
The running title appears to have been added by a different hand.
Black cross.

ab.w:
in
Quire signature at foot of page.
There is a cross at the top of the the page.
clinauit

ab.note: Black cross.
A number of pages are missing from the manuscript, containing John 21:7-24.
The final line is taken up with a decorative pattern.
